fields.name: Mechanics
fields.explain: Mechanics deals with the motion of objects and forces acting on them. It includes concepts like displacement, velocity, acceleration, force, power, work, momentum and laws of motion.

fields.name: Kinematics
fields.explain: Kinematics is branch of mechanics that describes motion without considering the cause of motion. It focuses on position, velocity, acceleration,etc.

fields.name: Dynamics
fields.explain: Dynamics studies motion along with the force causing it. It is based on Newton's Laws of Motion.

fields.name: Work, Energy and Power
fields.explain: This branch deals with how energy is transferred by forces and motion. It includes kinetic energy, potential energy, work done and power.

fields.name: Gravitation
fields.explain: Gravitation deals with force of attraction between masses and planetary motion.

fields.name: Thermodynamics
fields.explain: Thermodynamics deals with heat, temperature, energy transfer and laws governing thermal systems.

fields.name: Electrostatics
fields.explain: Electrostatics studies electric charges at rest and forces between them.

fields.name: Current Electricity
fields.explain: This branch focuses on flow of electric charges through conductors.

fields.name: Magnetism
fields.explain: Magnetism studies magnetic fields, force on moving charges and magnetic materials.

fields.name: Optics
fields.explain: Optics deals with behavior of light, including reflection, refraction and image formation.

fields.name: Modern Physics
fields.explain: Modern physics includes atomic structures, nuclear physics, photoelectric effect and relativity.

fields.name: Oscillations
fields.explain: Oscillation is a type of periodic motion in which body moves to and fro about a fixed mean position under action of restoring force.

fields.name: Electromagnetism
fields.explain: Electromagnetism is the fundamental physics describing the interaction between electric charges and magnetic fields.

fields.name: Quantum Physics
fields.explain: Quantum Physics is the fundamental physical theory that describes the behavior of matter and of light; its unusual characteristics typically occur at and below the scale of atoms

fields.name: Fluid Mechanics
fields.explain: Fluid mechanics is the branch of physics focused on how fluids respond to forces. It studies both liquids and gases, whether at rest (hydrostatics) or in motion (fluid dynamics).

fields.title: Potential Energy
fields.description: Energy stored in a body due to its position above ground
fields.question: Find the potential energy of a body of mass 3kg placed at a height of 10m
fields.solve: A ball of mass 2kg is lifted to a height of 5m above ground. Calculate its potential energy (g=9.8)
fields.form_info: m=Mass, g=Acceleration due to gravity, h=Height

fields.title: Ohm's Law
fields.description: Voltage(v) across conductor is directly proportional to the curent flowing through it
fields.question: Find the voltage across resistor of 5ohm when current is 2A
fields.solve: A resistor is connected to a battery of 12V. The current flowing through is 3A. Find the resistance.
fields.form_info: V=Voltage, I=Electric Current, R=Resistance

fields.title: Momentum
fields.description: It is the quantity of motion possessed by a body.A heavier or faster object has greater momentum
fields.question: Find the momentum of a body of mass 5kg moving with velocity 4m/s.
fields.solve: The Momentum achieved by a car of mass 400kg is 80kg*m/s. Calculate the speed at which the car is travelling.
fields.form_info: m=Mass, v=Velocity

fields.title: Newton's First Law of Motion
fields.description: If the net external force acting on a body at rest is zero, the body will remain at rest.
fields.question: A body of mass 2kg is moving with constant velocity of 5m/s on smooth horizontal surface. If the net external force acting on body is zero, find its velocity after 5s.
fields.solve: True of False: A body remains at rest unless and until external force acts on it.
fields.form_info: f=Force

fields.title: Gravitational Force
fields.description: Force of attraction between two masses.
fields.question: On what does Gravitational Force depends.
fields.solve: Relation between F(force) and r(distance) is?
fields.form_info: F=Force, G=Gravitational Constant, m=Mass, r=Distance between two masses.

fields.title: Escape Velocity
fields.description: Minimum Velocity(v) to escape Earth's gravity(gR)
fields.question: What is the escape velocity of earth.
fields.solve: Using Earth radius(R)=6400 km and g=9.8m/s^2. Calculate the escape velocity
fields.form_info: v=Escape Velocity, g=Acceleration due to gravity, R=Earth's radius.

fields.title: Coulomb's Law
fields.description: Force between two electric charges.
fields.question: Force between two electric charges depends on.
fields.solve: Let, the force between two protons of 5C and 2C be 20N. Find the distance between them , given: k=8.98*10^9 N*m^2/C^2.(take the initial value upto 1 decimal only)
fields.form_info: F=Force, q=Electric Charge, r=Distance between two charges., k=Coulomb's Constant(8.98*10^9)

fields.title: Electric Charge
fields.description: Charge flowing through a Conductor.
fields.question: Find the charge if I=2A for 5s.
fields.solve: Find the current for a conductor possessing charge of 20C for 50s.
fields.form_info: Q=Electric Charge, I=Electric Current, t=Time

fields.title: Electric Power
fields.description: Rate of electrical energy consumption.
fields.question: Find the power for V=10V and I=3A
fields.solve: The electrical energy consumed by a charged particle is 120W. Given that the electric current is 10A, find the Voltage provided.
fields.form_info: P=Electric Power, V=Voltage, I=Electric Current

fields.title: Time Period of Simple Pendulum
fields.description: Time Period depends only on length and gravity for small oscillations.
fields.question: Find Time Period for length 1m.
fields.solve: Find the length of a simple pendulum oscillating at 4s.(upto 2 decimals only)
fields.form_info: T=Time Period, π=pi(value=3.14), l=Length, g=Acceleration due to gravity(value=9.8)

fields.title: Heisenberg Uncertainity Principle
fields.description: The principle states that the exact positon and momentum of any moving particle cannot be known simultaneously.
fields.question: Find minimum uncertainty in position of a particle.
fields.solve: A proton with a mass of 1.67 x 10-27 kg  has an uncertainty in its velocity of 1.77 x 10^5 m/s.  Its uncertainty in position is B*10^-13 m. Given, h=6.63*10^-34, Π=3.14. Find B?(upto 2 decimals only)
fields.form_info: Δx= uncertainity in position, Δp= uncertainity in momentum, h=planck's constant.

fields.title: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
fields.description: Radiated Energy(E) is proportional to the fourth power of Temperature(T).
fields.question: Find energy radiated at T=100K. Given, σ=5.67 × 10-8 W/m2 k4.
fields.solve: The energy radiated from a particle is 8J. Find the Temperature, if σ=5.67 × 10-8 W/m2 k4.
fields.form_info: E=Radiated Energy, σ=Boltzmann constant, T=Temperature.

fields.title: Power
fields.description: Power(P) describes Rate of doing work(W) in a given time(t).
fields.question: Find power if work done is 100J in 4s.
fields.solve: The power given is 35W. Find the time in minutes if work done is 270J.(upto 3 decimals only)
fields.form_info: P=Power, W=Work, t=Time.

fields.title: Pressure
fields.description: Pressure(P) is defined as Force(F) applied per area(A).
fields.question: Find the pressure when F=67N, A=3m^2
fields.solve: The pressure acting on 2.5m^2 area of body is 24 Pa. Calculate the force applied.
fields.form_info: P=Pressure, F=Force, A=Area

fields.title: Ideal Gas Equation
fields.description: Relation between pressure(P), volume(V) and temperature(T) of the gas.
fields.question: Find the pressure when n=1 mol, T= 300K, V= 0.025 m^3, R=8.314 J/(K·mol)).
fields.solve: Find the mole(n) of a gas. Given, P= 4456 Pa, V= 3 m^3, T= 305K, R=8.314 J/(K·mol)). (only one integer value no decimal include)
fields.form_info: P=Pressure, V=Volume, n=mole, R=gas constant(8.314 J/(K·mol))), T=Temperature.

fields.title: Impulse
fields.description: Change in Momentum due to Force(F) applied.
fields.question: Given the Force of 20N, the change in time is 5s. Calculate the resultant Impulse(J).
fields.solve: The Change in Momentum due to 25N of Force is 60 Ns. Calculate the change in time?
fields.form_info: J=Impulse, F=Force ,  Δt=Change in time.

fields.title: Snell's Law
fields.description: Describes how light bends when passing from one medium into another.
fields.question: Light passes from air (n=1) to glass (n=1.5) at 60°. Find the refraction angle.
fields.solve: Light enters a medium from air (n=1) and incidents at 40° and refracts at 25°. Calculate the refractive index (n2) of that medium? (upto 2 decimal only).
fields.form_info: n1=Refractive index of medium 1,  θ1= Angle of incidence,  n2= Refractive index of medium 2,  θ2= Angle of refraction.

fields.title: Lens Formula
fields.description: Relates the focal length(f) of the mirror to object(u) and image distance(v).
fields.question: Object is 30cm from a concave mirror of focal length 10cm. Find the image distance?
fields.solve: The image distance formed after keeping an object at 25cm is 10cm. Calculate the focal length of that object.
fields.form_info: f= Focal length, v= Image distance, u= Object distance.

fields.title: Electric Potential Energy
fields.description: Potential energy(U) stored in a system of two point charges(q1 & q2).
fields.question: Two charges 1μC and 2μC are seperated by 0.1m. Calculate the potential energy?
fields.solve: Calculate the charge on a point with distance of 0.8m from a 4μC charge. Given U= 0.5J and k=8.99 × 10⁹ N·m²/C².(take the initial value upto 3 decimals).
fields.form_info: U= Potential energy, k=Coloumb's constant, q1 &q2= Electric charges.

fields.title: Capacitance
fields.description: Capacitance(C) is the ability of a body to store the electric charge(Q).
fields.question: A capacitor stores 6μC at 3V. Find it's Capacitance?
fields.solve: A charged body has 5μF of capacitance at 2V. It's electric charge is A × 10^-6, find A??
fields.form_info: C = Capacitance, Q = Electric charge, V = Voltage

fields.title: Magnetic force on a charge
fields.description: Force(F) on a charged particle(q) moving in a magnetic field(B).
fields.question: A 2C charge moves at 3m/s in a 4T field at 90°. Find the force on the charge?
fields.solve: The force on a 2.6m/s moving particle with 4C of charge is 17N. The Magnetic field produced was 6T, find the angle at which the particle interacted?  (take sinθ value upto 2 decimal only)
fields.form_info: F = Force, q = Electric charge, v = Velocity of particle, B = Magnetic field, θ = Angle of motion.

fields.title: Torque
fields.description: Torque is the tendency to rotate an object.
fields.question: A force of 20N acts at 0.5m at 90°. Find the torque?
fields.solve: The torque on an wheel is 25 Nm, while the force applied on that wheel at 60° is 17 N. Calculate the distance? (take upto 2 decimal only)
fields.form_info: 𝜏 = Torque, r = Distance, F = Force, θ = Angle of force

fields.title: Angular Momentum
fields.description: Angular Momentum(L) is rotational equivalent of Linear Momentum.
fields.question: A 2kg object moves in circle of radius 3m at 4m/s. Find its angular momentum?
fields.solve: The angular momentum of a circus wheel is 15 Kg m^2/s^2, the wheel is  20 kg with radius of 30 m. Calculate its speed?
fields.form_info: L = Angular Momentum, m = Mass, v = Velocity, r = Radius

fields.title: Carnot Efficiency
fields.description: Maximum possible efficiency of a heat engine operating at two different temperatures.
fields.question: A Carnot engine operates between 300K and 500K. Find the carnot efficiency?
fields.solve: The efficiency of an engine is 0.8. Find  the cold reservoir temperature if the hot reservoir temperature is 320K?
fields.form_info: η = Carnot efficiency, Tc = Temperature of cold reservoir, Th = Temperature of hot reservoir

fields.title: Relative Velocity
fields.description: The Velocity of Object A as observed from Object B.
fields.question: Car A moves at 60 km/h, Car B moves at 40 km/h in the same direction. Calculate the relative speed?
fields.solve: The relative velocity between two trains is 35 km/h. Train A moves at 50 km/h, find the velocity of Train B?
fields.form_info: v_AB= Relative velocity, v_A = Velocity of object A, v_B = Velocity of object B

fields.title: Maximum Height(Projectile)
fields.description: Maximum Vertical Height(H) reached by a projectile.
fields.question: A ball is projected at 20 m/s at 45°. Find it's max height?
fields.solve: A projectile is launched with velocity 20 m/s and reaches  a maximum height of 5 m. Find the angle of projection? (take g=10 m/s^2)
fields.form_info: H = Maximum height, u = Initial velocity, θ = Angle of projection, g = Acceleration due to gravity

fields.title: Friction Force
fields.description: Friction force(f) resists/opposes the motion of object(N) in opposite direction. The negative sign just displays that N and f are opposite to each other. No need to use negative sign in answer.
fields.question: A 5 kg of block on a surface with μ=0.3. Find the friction force. (g=10 m/s^2)
fields.solve: Given the coefficient of friction = 0.2 and the force to be 12 N. Find the mass of that object? (g = 10 m/s^2)
fields.form_info: f = Friction force, μ = Coefficient of friction, N = Normal Force.

fields.title: Elastic Potential Energy
fields.description: Energy stored in a compressed or stretched spring.
fields.question: A spring (k= 200 N/m) is stretched by 0.1 m. Find it's Potential energy?
fields.solve: The elastic potential energy stored in an spring is 2.5 J. Given (k=200 N/m), find the compression in string?
fields.form_info: PE= Elastic Potential Energy, k = Spring constant, x = compression/extension

fields.title: Time Period of a Satellite
fields.description: Time (T) taken by a Satellite to complete one full orbit.
fields.question: Finf T for a satellite at r=6400 Km, around earth. Given G=6.674 × 10⁻¹¹ m³⋅kg⁻¹⋅s⁻², M = 5.972 × 10²⁴ kg.
fields.form_info: T= Time taken, r = orbital radius, G  = Gravitational Constant, M = Mass

fields.title: Work done by Gas (Isobaric)
fields.description: Work done(W) by the gas when Pressure(P) remains constant.
fields.question: Gas expands at P = 2 × 10^5 Pa,  ΔV = 0.5 m^3. Find the Work done?
fields.form_info: W = Work done, P = Pressure,  ΔV = Change in Volume.

fields.title: Kirchoff's Voltage Law
fields.description: Sum of all voltages in a closed loop is always zero.
fields.question: In a loop: battery 12V, R1 = 4 Ω  , R2 = 2 Ω. Find the current?
fields.form_info: V = Voltage

fields.title: Kirchoff's Curent Law
fields.description: Total Current entering a junction equals total current leaving it.
fields.question: 3A and 4A currents enter a junction. If 1A leaves from one branch, find the current leaving from other branch?
fields.form_info: I_in = Curent entering, I_out = Current leaving

fields.title: Wheatstone Bridge
fields.description: Condition for a balanced Wheatstone  bridge (no current through galvanometer)
fields.question: P = 10Ω, Q = 20Ω, R = 5Ω. Find S?
fields.form_info: P,Q,R,S = resistances in bridge circuit

fields.title: Power Dissipated
fields.description: Electrical Power converted into heat in a resistor
fields.question: A 4Ω resistor carries 3A. Find the power dissipated?
fields.form_info: P =Power dissipated, I = Current, R = Resistor

fields.title: Force on Current-carrying  Conductor
fields.description: Magnetic force experienced by a current-carrying conductor in magnetic field.
fields.question: A 0.5 m wire carries 3A in B = 2T field at 90°. Find the force?
fields.form_info: F = Force, B = Magnetic Field(T), I = Current, L = Length of wire, θ = Angle

fields.title: Magnetic Field of Solenoid
fields.description: Uniform magnetic field inside a long solenoid
fields.question: A Solenoid has 1000 turns/m carrying 2A. Find B inside. Given μ₀  = 4π x 10⁻⁷ H/m
fields.form_info: B = Magnetic Field, μ₀ = Permeability of free space, n = Turns per minute, I =Current

fields.title: Energy stored in Inductor
fields.description: Energy stored in the magnetic field of an inductor
fields.question: L = 2H, I=3A. Find the Energy stored?
fields.form_info: U = Energy stored, L = Inductance (H) , I = Current

fields.title: Photoelectric Effect
fields.description: Maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons ejected from a metal surface.
fields.question: Light of f = 8 × 10^11 Hz hits the metal surface of (Φ  = 2 eV). Find the KE, Given h = 6.62607015 × 10⁻³⁴ J.s
fields.form_info: KE = Kinetic Energy, h = Planck's Constant, f  = Frequency,  Φ = Work function

fields.title: Mass-Energy Equivalence
fields.description: A small amount of Mass(m) can generate Large amount of Energy(E)
fields.question: Find energy equivalent to 1 g of matter. Given c = 3 × 10^8 m/s 3
fields.form_info: E =Energy, m =Mass, c = Speed of Light

fields.title: Bohr's Energy (nth Orbit)
fields.description: Energy of an electron in nth orbit of hydrogen atom.
fields.question: Find the energy of electron in n=2 orbit of hydrogen?
fields.form_info: E = Energy, n = Principle Quantum number(1,2,3....)

fields.title: Radioactive Decay Law
fields.description: Number of radioactive nuclei remaining  after time t.
fields.question: N_0 = 1000, λ = 0.1 s^-1. Find N after 10 s?
fields.form_info: N = Remaining nuclei after time t,  N_0 = Initial nuclei, λ = Decay Constant, t = Time

fields.title: Half-Life
fields.description: Time required  for half the radioactive nuclei to decay
fields.question: A substance has λ = 0.02 s^-1. Find the half-life
fields.form_info: λ = Decay constant
